title: Create durable packets for AMP266 through AMP300
persona: Operations, strategy, control, planning, data, and executive reviewers
steps: Open representative AMP266-AMP300 workspaces from the generated Assistant subnav.
Review source signals, risk assessment, recommendations, artifact, approval route, and rollback plan.
expected: Each AMP266-AMP300 workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with a distinct ampNumber and programKey.
Each packet has a domain-specific nav label, title, artifact, approval owners, and no-mutation scope.
The shared advanced-program UI displays preview metrics, packet history, review notes, and queue-review controls.

Policies, decisions, operating cadences, SLA credits, critical command records, lane designs, inventory pools, sourcing architectures, digital threads, twin models, calibration settings, process maps, maturity scorecards, capability maps, and value-stream execution plans are not mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for AMP266 through AMP300 packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT packet.
Add a review note describing the downstream decision.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
AssistantActionQueueItem uses an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp266_review or assistant_amp300_review.
AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream records were not changed automatically.

title: Governance, command, digital-thread, and value-stream packets preserve no-silent-mutation guardrails
steps: Open a packet with active risk signals.
Inspect recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Queue review without executing downstream changes.
expected: recommendationJson requires review before policy, operating model, critical-command, digital-thread, twin, process, or value-stream execution.
approvalPlanJson names the responsible owner groups.
rollbackPlanJson preserves source records and requires a fresh packet when evidence changes materially.
Autonomous control-plane, decision-memory, and process-improvement recommendations remain review-only and cannot silently expand automation.
